title: Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement
description: Two-party mutual NDA suitable for exploratory commercial discussions.
body:
# MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is made on {{effective_date}} between:

**{{party_a}}**, having its registered office at {{party_a_addr}} ("Party A");

and

**{{party_b}}**, having its registered office at {{party_b_addr}} ("Party B");

each a "Party" and together the "Parties".

## 1. PURPOSE

The Parties wish to exchange confidential information in connection with {{purpose}} (the "Purpose"). This Agreement sets out the terms on which such information will be disclosed and used.

## 2. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

"Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by one Party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") in connection with the Purpose, whether orally, in writing, or in any other form, that is identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure.

## 3. OBLIGATIONS

The Receiving Party shall:

(a) use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose;

(b) protect the Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in no event less than a reasonable standard of care;

(c) disclose the Confidential Information only to its employees, advisors, and contractors who need to know it for the Purpose and who are bound by written obligations of confidentiality no less protective than those in this Agreement; and

(d) not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any tangible Confidential Information.

## 4. EXCLUSIONS

The obligations in Clause 3 shall not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; (b) was lawfully in the Receiving Party's possession without obligation of confidence prior to disclosure, as evidenced by its written records; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information; or (d) is lawfully obtained from a third party with the right to disclose it.

## 5. COMPELLED DISCLOSURE

If the Receiving Party is compelled by law, court order, or regulatory authority to disclose Confidential Information, it shall, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order. The Receiving Party shall disclose only the portion of Confidential Information legally required.

## 6. TERM

This Agreement shall commence on the date first above written and shall remain in force for a period of {{term_years}} years, unless terminated earlier by either Party on thirty (30) days' written notice. The obligations in Clause 3 shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of {{term_years}} years from the date of disclosure.

## 7. RETURN OF INFORMATION

On the written request of the Disclosing Party, or on termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or, at the Disclosing Party's option, destroy all Confidential Information in its possession or control, and certify such destruction in writing.

## 8. NO LICENCE OR WARRANTY

Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting the Receiving Party any licence or right in respect of the Confidential Information, except as expressly set out in this Agreement. The Confidential Information is provided "as is" and the Disclosing Party makes no warranty as to its accuracy or completeness.

## 9. REMEDIES

The Parties acknowledge that a breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be inadequate. Accordingly, the non-breaching Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief and other equitable remedies, in addition to any other remedies available at law.

## 10. GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of {{governing_law}}. The courts at {{seat}} shall have exclusive jurisdiction over any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

## 11. ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to its subject matter and supersedes all prior negotiations and understandings.

---

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have caused this Agreement to be executed by their duly authorised representatives as of the date first above written.

**For {{party_a}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________
Signature: ______________________

**For {{party_b}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________
Signature: ______________________

title: SaaS Subscription Agreement (Lite)
description: A concise SaaS subscription agreement for standalone software services. Use an Order Form for commercials.
body:
# SAAS SUBSCRIPTION AGREEMENT

This SaaS Subscription Agreement (the "Agreement") is made on {{effective_date}} between:

**{{supplier}}**, having its registered office at {{supplier_addr}} (the "Supplier"); and

**{{customer}}**, having its registered office at {{customer_addr}} (the "Customer").

## 1. DEFINITIONS

"Services" means the cloud-based software-as-a-service solution identified as {{service_name}}, including any updates, patches, and documentation.

"Authorised Users" means the Customer's employees, contractors, and agents authorised by the Customer to use the Services under this Agreement.

"Order Form" means any ordering document entered into between the parties that references this Agreement and sets out the commercial terms.

"Personal Data" has the meaning given in the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 of India and, where applicable, Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

## 2. LICENCE

Subject to the Customer's payment of all applicable fees and compliance with this Agreement, the Supplier grants the Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, worldwide right during the Subscription Term to access and use the Services solely for the Customer's internal business operations and only through its Authorised Users.

The Customer shall not: (a) reverse engineer or decompile the Services; (b) modify or create derivative works; (c) resell, sublicense, or make the Services available to third parties; (d) use the Services to build a competitive product; or (e) remove proprietary notices.

## 3. TERM

This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an initial term of {{initial_term}} months (the "Initial Term"). Thereafter, it shall automatically renew for successive twelve (12) month periods (each a "Renewal Term") unless either party gives written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current term.

## 4. FEES AND PAYMENT

The Customer shall pay the Supplier subscription fees of {{fees_currency}} {{fees}} per month, exclusive of applicable taxes. The Supplier shall invoice monthly in advance. All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Late payments shall accrue interest at the rate of one and a half per cent (1.5%) per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is lower.

## 5. SERVICE LEVEL

The Supplier shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain monthly Service availability of at least {{uptime}}%, excluding scheduled maintenance, emergency maintenance, Force Majeure Events, and issues caused by the Customer or third parties outside the Supplier's control.

## 6. DATA PROTECTION

Where the Supplier processes Personal Data on behalf of the Customer, it shall act as a Data Processor / Processor and the Customer as the Data Fiduciary / Controller. The Supplier shall: (a) process Personal Data only on the Customer's documented instructions; (b) implement appropriate technical and organisational measures under Section 8 of the DPDPA 2023 and, where applicable, Article 32 GDPR; (c) notify the Customer without undue delay, and in any event within seventy-two (72) hours, of any Personal Data breach; (d) not engage sub-processors without the Customer's prior written consent; and (e) return or delete Personal Data on termination. A separate Data Processing Addendum, if executed, shall prevail over this Clause to the extent of any conflict.

## 7. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each party shall keep confidential all non-public information disclosed by the other party and shall use it solely to perform its obligations under this Agreement. Standard exclusions apply for publicly available, independently developed, or pre-existing information. This obligation shall survive termination for three (3) years.

## 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

The Supplier retains all right, title, and interest in and to the Services. The Customer retains all right, title, and interest in and to Customer Data. The Customer grants the Supplier a limited, worldwide licence to use Customer Data solely to provide the Services.

## 9. WARRANTIES

The Supplier warrants that the Services shall be performed in a professional manner in accordance with industry standards and the documentation. Except as expressly set out herein, the Services are provided "as is" and all implied warranties are disclaimed to the maximum extent permitted by law.

## 10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

Except for the Excluded Liabilities (breach of confidentiality, indemnification obligations, IP infringement, wilful misconduct, and liabilities that cannot be limited under applicable law), each party's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the liability. Neither party shall be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, revenue, or data.

## 11. INDEMNIFICATION

The Supplier shall defend and indemnify the Customer against third-party claims that the Services infringe any IP rights, subject to standard carve-outs (unauthorised modifications, combinations with third-party products, use outside scope). The Customer shall indemnify the Supplier against claims arising from Customer Data or the Customer's use of the Services in violation of this Agreement or applicable law.

## 12. TERMINATION

Either party may terminate this Agreement: (a) for material breach by the other party that remains uncured thirty (30) days after written notice; or (b) if the other party becomes insolvent or enters into insolvency proceedings. Upon termination, all licences cease, the Customer shall pay accrued fees, and the Supplier shall make Customer Data available for export for thirty (30) days, after which it shall be deleted.

## 13. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of {{governing_law}}. Any dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts at {{seat}}, subject to any arbitration provisions separately agreed.

## 14. GENERAL

This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. No amendment shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties. If any provision is held invalid, the remainder shall remain in force. Neither party may assign this Agreement without the other's consent, except to an affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.

---

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have caused this Agreement to be executed by their duly authorised representatives as of the date first above written.

**For {{supplier}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________
Signature: ______________________

**For {{customer}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________
Signature: ______________________

title: Consulting Services Agreement
description: Master services agreement for consulting engagements with SOW-based workstreams.
body:
# CONSULTING SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Consulting Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is made on {{effective_date}} between:

**{{consultant}}**, at {{consultant_addr}} (the "Consultant"); and

**{{client}}**, at {{client_addr}} (the "Client").

## 1. ENGAGEMENT

The Client engages the Consultant to provide consulting services (the "Services") as described in one or more statements of work (each, a "SOW") entered into and executed by the parties, which shall reference and be governed by this Agreement.

## 2. SERVICES

The Consultant shall perform the Services in accordance with each SOW, in a professional and workmanlike manner, using personnel with appropriate skills, training, and experience. The Consultant shall comply with all applicable laws and with the Client's reasonable policies and procedures made known to the Consultant in writing.

## 3. FEES AND EXPENSES

3.1 The fee structure for the Services shall be {{fee_structure}}, as further detailed in each SOW.

3.2 Where the Services are provided on a time and materials basis, the Consultant's hourly rate shall be {{hourly_rate}}, subject to escalation annually on written notice.

3.3 Reasonable and pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses shall be reimbursed by the Client against receipts.

3.4 The Consultant shall invoice monthly in arrears. All invoices are due within thirty (30) days of the invoice date. Amounts are exclusive of applicable taxes, including GST.

## 4. INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR

The Consultant is an independent contractor. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, employment, or agency relationship. Neither party shall have authority to bind the other.

## 5. OWNERSHIP OF WORK PRODUCT

All work product created specifically for the Client under this Agreement (the "Deliverables") shall be the property of the Client upon full payment of fees. The Consultant assigns all right, title, and interest in the Deliverables, including associated IP rights. The Consultant retains ownership of pre-existing tools, methodologies, and templates, and grants the Client a perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive licence to use them as incorporated in the Deliverables.

## 6. CONFIDENTIALITY

Each party shall protect Confidential Information of the other with reasonable care, use it only to perform this Agreement, and disclose it only on a need-to-know basis. Standard exclusions apply. Obligations survive termination for three (3) years.

## 7. DATA PROTECTION

Where the Consultant processes Personal Data on behalf of the Client, it shall comply with applicable data protection laws, including the DPDPA 2023 of India and, where applicable, the GDPR, and shall process such Personal Data solely for the purposes of performing the Services and in accordance with the Client's written instructions.

## 8. NON-SOLICITATION

During the Term and for twelve (12) months thereafter, neither party shall directly solicit for employment any personnel of the other who were directly involved in the performance of the Services, excluding general recruitment advertising not targeted at such individuals.

## 9. WARRANTIES AND DISCLAIMERS

The Consultant warrants that the Services will be performed professionally and in conformity with the SOW. The Consultant's sole obligation for breach of this warranty is to re-perform the affected Services. All other warranties, express or implied, are disclaimed to the maximum extent permitted by law.

## 10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

Except for breach of confidentiality, IP indemnification, and wilful misconduct, each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed the fees paid by the Client under the relevant SOW in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim. Neither party shall be liable for indirect or consequential damages.

## 11. TERM AND TERMINATION

This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated. Either party may terminate: (a) for convenience on thirty (30) days' written notice; (b) immediately for material breach uncured within thirty (30) days of notice; or (c) immediately for insolvency. Termination of this Agreement shall terminate all active SOWs, unless the parties agree otherwise in writing. The Client shall pay for all Services properly performed up to the effective date of termination.

## 12. GOVERNING LAW

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of {{governing_law}}. Any dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts at {{seat}}, subject to any arbitration provisions separately agreed.

## 13. GENERAL

This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties. No amendment shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.

---

**For {{consultant}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________

**For {{client}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________

title: Data Processing Addendum (DPDPA + GDPR)
description: Standalone DPA addendum suitable for attaching to an existing SaaS or services agreement.

fields.label: Categories of Personal Data
fields.placeholder: Name, email, billing address, IP address

fields.label: Categories of Data Subjects
fields.placeholder: Employees and customers of the Controller

fields.label: Processing purpose
fields.placeholder: Provision of the Services under the main agreement

body:
# DATA PROCESSING ADDENDUM

This Data Processing Addendum (the "DPA") is entered into on {{effective_date}} between **{{controller}}** (the "Controller") and **{{processor}}** (the "Processor"), and supplements {{main_agreement}} (the "Main Agreement"). In the event of any conflict between this DPA and the Main Agreement regarding the processing of Personal Data, this DPA shall prevail.

## 1. DEFINITIONS

Capitalised terms used but not defined in this DPA have the meanings given in the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 of India ("DPDPA") and Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR"), as applicable. "Applicable Data Protection Laws" means the DPDPA, the GDPR, and any other data protection or privacy laws applicable to the processing of Personal Data under this DPA.

## 2. SCOPE AND PURPOSE

The Processor shall process the following Personal Data on behalf of the Controller:

- **Categories of Personal Data**: {{data_categories}}
- **Categories of Data Subjects / Data Principals**: {{data_subjects}}
- **Purpose of processing**: {{processing_purpose}}
- **Duration of processing**: the term of the Main Agreement

## 3. PROCESSOR OBLIGATIONS

The Processor shall:

(a) process Personal Data only on the documented instructions of the Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country, unless required to do so by applicable law to which the Processor is subject, in which case the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before processing, unless prohibited by law;

(b) ensure that persons authorised to process Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality;

(c) implement and maintain appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, as described in Annexure A to this DPA;

(d) respect the conditions for engaging sub-processors as set out in Clause 5;

(e) assist the Controller, by appropriate technical and organisational measures, in fulfilling the Controller's obligation to respond to requests from Data Subjects / Data Principals exercising their rights under Applicable Data Protection Laws;

(f) assist the Controller in ensuring compliance with security, breach notification, and data protection impact assessment obligations;

(g) at the Controller's choice, delete or return all Personal Data at the end of the provision of services, and delete existing copies unless applicable law requires storage; and

(h) make available to the Controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA and allow for and contribute to audits, including inspections, conducted by the Controller or an auditor mandated by the Controller.

## 4. PERSONAL DATA BREACH

The Processor shall notify the Controller without undue delay, and in any event within seventy-two (72) hours, after becoming aware of a Personal Data Breach. The notification shall describe the nature of the breach, the categories and approximate number of Data Subjects and records affected, the likely consequences, the measures taken or proposed to address it, and the contact details of the Processor's point of contact. The Processor shall provide reasonable assistance to the Controller in complying with its notification obligations to supervisory authorities and Data Subjects.

## 5. SUB-PROCESSORS

The Controller grants the Processor general authorisation to engage sub-processors from the list maintained by the Processor and made available to the Controller on request. The Processor shall give the Controller at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice of any intended change concerning the addition or replacement of sub-processors, during which the Controller may object on reasonable grounds. The Processor shall impose on each sub-processor, by written contract, the same data protection obligations as are imposed on the Processor under this DPA, and shall remain fully liable to the Controller for the performance of each sub-processor.

## 6. INTERNATIONAL TRANSFERS

Where Personal Data is transferred outside India or the European Economic Area, the parties shall ensure that such transfers are subject to appropriate safeguards in accordance with Applicable Data Protection Laws, including, where required, the Standard Contractual Clauses issued by the European Commission or equivalent instruments.

## 7. DATA SUBJECT RIGHTS

The Processor shall promptly notify the Controller of any request received directly from a Data Subject / Data Principal and shall not respond to such request without the Controller's prior written authorisation, except to the extent necessary to redirect the Data Subject to the Controller or as required by applicable law.

## 8. RECORDS OF PROCESSING

The Processor shall maintain written records of all processing activities carried out on behalf of the Controller, containing the information required by Applicable Data Protection Laws, and shall make such records available to supervisory authorities on request.

## 9. TERM AND TERMINATION

This DPA shall remain in force for the duration of the Main Agreement. On termination of the Main Agreement, the Processor shall, at the Controller's option, return or delete all Personal Data within thirty (30) days, and provide written certification of deletion.

## 10. LIABILITY

The limitations of liability set out in the Main Agreement shall apply to claims arising under this DPA, except to the extent prohibited by Applicable Data Protection Laws.

---

## ANNEXURE A — TECHNICAL AND ORGANISATIONAL MEASURES

The Processor shall implement and maintain the following measures:

1. **Access control** — role-based access, least privilege, multi-factor authentication for administrative access.
2. **Encryption** — Personal Data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256 or equivalent).
3. **Network security** — firewalls, intrusion detection, network segmentation.
4. **Secure development** — secure SDLC practices aligned with OWASP ASVS or equivalent.
5. **Monitoring and logging** — centralised logging, regular log review, anomaly detection.
6. **Personnel** — background checks, data protection training, confidentiality commitments.
7. **Business continuity** — regular backups, tested disaster recovery, documented incident response.
8. **Audit** — annual independent security audits (e.g. SOC 2 Type II or ISO 27001), results available on request.

---

**For {{controller}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________

**For {{processor}}:**

Name: ______________________
Title: ______________________
